name: Lumen Technologies Network & API Platform
what_it_does: Lumen Technologies REST API for enterprise networking, connectivity, and network-as-a-service capabilities. Enables AI agents to manage network bandwidth and circuit provisioning for enterprise connectivity management, handle SD-WAN policy and configuration management for enterprise network automation, access network performance monitoring and SLA reporting for network operations, retrieve circuit order management and service activation workflow for network procurement, manage DDoS mitigation and security service configuration for network security, handle CDN (Content Delivery Network) management and configuration for content delivery optimization, access voice and unified communications service management for enterprise communication, retrieve edge compute and network edge service management for distributed application deployment, manage IP transit and internet connectivity services, and integrate Lumen network data with ITSM, SD-WAN, and enterprise IT management platforms.
use_cases: Provisioning network circuits from connectivity management agents
Managing SD-WAN policies from network automation agents
Monitoring network performance from NOC automation agents
Configuring DDoS mitigation from network security agents
not_for: Consumer internet service without enterprise networking focus
Small businesses without enterprise network service requirements
Last-mile consumer broadband without carrier-grade network services
best_when: An enterprise network operations team or MSP using Lumen for carrier services wants AI agents to automate network provisioning, SD-WAN management, performance monitoring, and security service management.
avoid_when: NETWORK AVAILABILITY RISK: Lumen network service automation affects enterprise production network connectivity — automated circuit configuration changes without change management approval can cause network outages. SD-WAN policy automation without testing creates traffic routing failures. Lumen (formerly CenturyLink, formerly Level 3) has had significant business transformation and rebranding; API stability and product consolidation ongoing. Lumen went through chapter 11 bankruptcy restructuring (2023); verify current service availability and API support.

auth.notes: Lumen uses OAuth 2.0. Developer portal at developer.lumen.com. Webhooks for network events. REST API with JSON. Monroe, Louisiana HQ. Public (LUMN NYSE). CenturyLink rebranded to Lumen Technologies (2020). Acquired Level 3 Communications (2017, $34B). Lumen filed Chapter 11 bankruptcy for subsidiary (2023) — corporate restructuring ongoing. 4th largest US telecom. Competes with AT&T, Verizon, and Zayo for enterprise network services.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — contract-based enterprise pricing
pricing.paid_tiers: Annual contract; per-circuit and per-service pricing
pricing.notes: Monroe, Louisiana. Lumen Technologies (LUMN NYSE). CenturyLink rebranded 2020. Level 3 acquisition 2017. Chapter 11 subsidiary restructuring 2023. 4th largest US telecom. Enterprise networking focus. Competes with AT&T and Verizon for enterprise connectivity.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth client; documented per enterprise contract

known_agent_gotchas: NETWORK AVAILABILITY RISK: Automated circuit and network configuration changes require change management approval; unauthorized automated changes can cause enterprise production network outages
Chapter 11 restructuring uncertainty — Lumen's 2023 Chapter 11 filing for subsidiary creates product and API support uncertainty; verify current Lumen service commitment before long-term integration
CenturyLink/Level 3 legacy system complexity — Lumen operates across legacy CenturyLink and Level 3 systems post-merger; API coverage and consistency varies by legacy platform
SD-WAN policy automation — automated SD-WAN policy changes without sandbox testing can create traffic routing failures in production enterprise networks
Circuit provisioning lead times — network circuit provisioning has physical lead times (days to weeks); automated provisioning requests must account for non-instant fulfillment
DDoS mitigation automation — automated DDoS mitigation changes must not block legitimate traffic; automated rate limiting configuration requires careful threshold management
agent_readiness.security_notes: Enterprise networking. SOC2, ISO27001. OAuth2. US. Network service and configuration data.

security_score_components.security_notes: Enterprise networking. SOC2, ISO27001. OAuth2. US. Network and connectivity data.
